title: Clitandra leptantha

intro: Clitandra leptantha is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, native to tropical Africa. Traditionally, Clitandra leptantha has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and malaria, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Clitandra leptantha include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. In herbal medicine, Clitandra leptantha is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the form of a tincture or extract to treat various health conditions.
